title: The Founder and CEO of Prologis on Staying Ahead of Disruptive Change
content:
The greatest advantage any leader or organization can have today is the ability to anticipate change. At Prologis, the world's largest real-estate-investment trust, we have a proven history of getting ahead of client needs. Over the past four decades, we've navigated the rise of e-commerce, global trade surges, and the need for new services. To achieve this, we follow a simple playbook: listen to customers, position yourself to act, experiment, and then invest. Listening to customers is arguably the most critical step in this process.

If you ask clients the right questions and pay attention to their actions, they will tell you exactly what they need. For example, many simply ask us to make their lives easier, driving us to expand into ancillary services like energy and sustainability. Positioning yourself requires financial strength, and maintaining a strong balance sheet gives you the flexibility to act fast. This enables you to invest in new ventures and support customers when they need it most. Furthermore, experimentation is essential because we must always think about what comes next.

We continually run low-cost trials to test our intuitions about the market's direction. For example, we're currently exploring the conversion of warehouses into low-latency data centers to power the AI age. The market will eventually tell you if you have a right to play in new areas, but you must take the first step. Finally, you have to trust your results and invest heavily in what genuinely works. Long-term success doesn't come from coasting; it comes from future-focused evolution.
